title: CMU 15-445/645 Fall 2024 — Lecture 8: B+Tree Indexes
author: Andy Pavlo, Carnegie Mellon University
note: Lecture slides covering B+Tree structure, insert/delete/split operations, and design choices like node size and variable-length keys — a deeper treatment of the chapter's B-tree section.

title: The Log-Structured Merge-Tree (LSM-Tree)
author: Patrick O'Neil, Edward Cheng, Dieter Gawlick, Elizabeth O'Neil
note: The original 1996 paper that introduced the LSM-tree, motivating deferred, batched index writes for insert-heavy workloads — the direct ancestor of the storage engines in this chapter.

title: Bigtable: A Distributed Storage System for Structured Data (OSDI 2006)
author: Fay Chang, Jeffrey Dean, Sanjay Ghemawat, et al. (Google)
note: The paper that coined the terms SSTable and memtable; section 4-6 show how a real system combines a commit log, in-memory buffer, and immutable sorted files with compaction.

title: RocksDB Wiki: Leveled Compaction
author: RocksDB team (Meta)
note: Official documentation of how a production LSM engine organizes SSTables into size-tiered levels and picks files to compact — concrete detail behind the chapter's compaction and write-amplification discussion.

title: Lightweight Compression in DuckDB
author: Mark Raasveldt (DuckDB)
note: Engineering blog explaining why columnar layouts compress so well, walking through RLE, bit-packing, frame-of-reference, dictionary, and FSST encodings used in a real analytical engine.

title: Everything You Always Wanted to Know About Compiled and Vectorized Queries But Were Afraid to Ask
author: Timo Kersten, Viktor Leis, Alfons Kemper, Thomas Neumann, et al.
note: VLDB 2018 paper that empirically compares query compilation (HyPer-style) with vectorized execution (VectorWise-style) — the definitive reference for the chapter's query-execution section.

title: Elasticsearch from the Bottom Up, Part 1
author: Alex Brasetvik (Elastic)
note: Builds up Lucene's inverted index from term dictionaries and postings lists to immutable segments and merging — a clear bridge from the chapter's SSTable ideas to full-text search.

title: Hierarchical Navigable Small Worlds (HNSW)
author: Pinecone (Faiss: The Missing Manual series)
note: Visual explainer of the HNSW graph index for approximate nearest-neighbor vector search, with Faiss code and recall/speed trade-off benchmarks for the chapter's vector-embedding section.
